title: Dates and sorting by newest
question: Should decisions carry a date, and can the list be sorted newest-first without losing the project-stage grouping?
rationale: Stamp each decision with a date automatically and add a 'By stage / Recent' switch. 'Recent' sorts by last activity so a just-revised decision rises instead of staying buried under its original date — and Recent is now the default view, so the list opens on what changed most recently; By phase is one menu choice away.

history.from: Recent sorted by original decision date only
history.reason: A revised decision stayed buried under its creation date, which undercut the 'what changed lately' job. Recent now sorts by last activity (max of the decision's date and its revision dates).

history.from: The card and list row showed the original created date
history.reason: The card/row now shows the last-revised date (the same last-activity date the Recent sort uses), so the list reflects how current each decision is; the original created date still shows when you open the decision's detail.

history.from: By phase was the default list grouping; Recent was opt-in via the switch (d6 framework first)
history.reason: Made Recent the default view so the list opens on what changed most recently — matching d18, which already opens the detail panel on the most-recent decision; By phase is now one choice away in the Sort menu (d30 revised)

options.label: Add dates plus a 'By stage / Recent' switch

tradeoffs.note: keeps stage grouping as the default view (d6 intact)
newest-first only when asked; undated items sink to the bottom
dates fill in going forward; the older 6 stay undated

options.label: Replace stage grouping with a date timeline

tradeoffs.note: drops the stage framework that d6 deliberately chose

options.label: No dates, no sorting (leave as is)

tradeoffs.note: no sense of when or in what order decisions were made
